input_dict.question: What was the record for the head football coach of this private four-year Christian-based college founded in Salina, Kansas, United States?
input_dict.source_summary: The college is Kansas Wesleyan University. Passages list several Kansas Wesleyan head-football-coach records (Coach Miller 0–1 in 1906; W. F. Ragle 5–4 in 1915; C. L. Williams 9–5 in 1908–1909; Ron Dupree 11–18 for his Kansas Wesleyan tenure), and note Matt Drinkall was hired in 2014 but give no win–loss record for him, so it’s unclear which single "head coach" record the question intends.
input_dict.target_summary: The institution is Kansas Wesleyan University. Available coach records from the provided inputs: Coach Miller — served one season (1906), compiling a record of 0–1; W. F. Ragle — served one season (1915), compiling a record of 5–4; C. L. Williams — served two seasons (1908–1909), compiling a record of 9–5. The inputs also state Matt Drinkall was hired as Kansas Wesleyan head coach in January 2014, but no win–loss record for him is provided.
input_dict.candidate_summary: The institution is Kansas Wesleyan University. Available coach records from the provided inputs: Coach Miller — served one season (1906), compiling a record of 0–1; W.
